items.prompt: In Example 2.16, why is a full-featured survey platform chosen over a bare email form?
choices: Email forms cannot collect any responses
Branching logic and CSV export into the ticket warehouse are required
The study needs browser automation for HTML catalogs
Probability sampling is impossible on tablets
items.explain: Tool choice follows workflow needs—conditional items and export integration—not layout polish alone.

items.prompt: When should a team switch from a static HTML parser to a browser driver?
choices: When titles load only after user interaction such as clicking a term filter
Only for API endpoints with JSON payloads
When the frame is alphabetically ordered
items.explain: Example 2.17 chooses Selenium-class drivers when content is not present in the initial HTML.

items.prompt: Survey platforms can fix poorly designed questionnaire items without cognitive testing.
items.explain: Platforms encode workflow; clinical-grade item design still sits outside any form builder's defaults.

choices: Scrapy — primary vital signs at bedside
BeautifulSoup — only real-time IoT edge pipelines
items.explain: Table 2.6 maps survey platforms to questionnaire methods, scrapers to web harvesting, and API clients to structured feeds.

items.prompt: Why should tool selection come after method and instrument design?
items.rubric: Tools implement choices; they do not define research design or sampling.
items.sample_answer: Platforms and libraries operationalize an already chosen method and instrument; they cannot substitute for objectives, sampling, or item design.
